title: ParallelComp_Sub
text:
library(parallel)
library(foreach)
library(doParallel)

Loading required package: iterators



Question 1

exp_data <- foreach(i = 1:100, .combine = "rbind") %do% {
  data = rexp(20,1) #generate 100 samples of size 20 from exp(1)
  means <- mean(data)
  vars <- var(data)
  c(mean = means, variance = vars)
}

head(exp_data)

              mean  variance
result.1 0.7907379 0.4896164
result.2 0.9532241 0.8613787
result.3 0.6721674 0.6372440
result.4 1.1700787 2.1552881
result.5 1.1135454 0.6474052
result.6 0.8777339 0.5307872




Question 2

library(MASS)

data(galaxies)
data <- galaxies

B <- 10000
cl <- makeCluster(3)
registerDoParallel(cl)

#Parallel Computing Time
start <- Sys.time()
bstr <- foreach(i = 1:B, .combine = 'c') %dopar% {
  bstr_sample = sample(data, replace = 'TRUE')
  bstr_med = median(bstr_sample)
}
end <- Sys.time()
par_time <- end-start
cat("Parallel Comp Time:", par_time)

Parallel Comp Time: 4.62939

#head(bstr)

stopCluster(cl)


B <- 10000
start <- Sys.time()
bstr <- foreach(i = 1:B) %do% {
  bstr_sample = sample(data, replace = 'TRUE')
  bstr_med = median(bstr_sample)
}
end <- Sys.time()
non_partime <- end - start
cat("Non Parallel Computing time:", non_partime)

Non Parallel Computing time: 3.358484



#Bootstrap with many "workers"
cl <- makeCluster(10)
registerDoParallel(cl)

system.time({
  serial <- numeric(B)
  foreach(i = 1:B, .combine = 'c') %do%{
  median(sample(data, replace = TRUE)) }
})

   user  system elapsed 
   4.08    0.07    4.21 




Question 3

exp_data <- rexp(50, 1) #population
true_mean <- mean(exp_data)
num_sims <- 1000
B <- 1000
alpha <- 0.05

count <- 0

for (i in 1:num_sims){
  samp <- sample(exp_data, 50, replace = TRUE) #bootstrap samples 
  boot_means <- numeric(B)
  
  for (b in 1:B){
    bs_sample <- sample(samp, 50, replace = TRUE)
    boot_means[b] <- mean(bs_sample)
    
  }
  
  ci <- quantile(boot_means, probs = c(alpha/2, 1-alpha/2))
  
  if (true_mean >= ci[1] & true_mean <= ci[2]){
    count = count + 1
  }
}
  
cov_rate <- (count/num_sims)*100
cat("Coverage of percentile bootstrap CI:", cov_rate,"%")

Coverage of percentile bootstrap CI: 93.6 %




Question 4
Find largest value in each vector, iterating over the vectors (3 of them, containing 5 each)

vec1 <- c(2,5,6,8,4)
vec2 <- c(2,7,6,78,4)
vec3 <- c(2,35,6,87,4)
vec_list <- list(vec1, vec2, vec3)


#using for each 
result <- function(vector){
  foreach(i=vec_list) %do% {
  max_num <- max(i)
  #print(max_num)
  }
}


#Creating the vectors using the irnorm
#installed.packages("iterators")
library(iterators)

set.seed(1234)
results_seq <- foreach(i = irnorm(n= 5, mean =0, sd =1, count =3)) %do% {
  max(i)
}
print(results_seq)

[[1]]
[1] 1.084441

[[2]]
[1] 0.5060559

[[3]]
[1] 0.9594941




Question 5

#RunTime foreach 
start1 <- Sys.time()
results_seq <- foreach(i = irnorm(n= 5, mean =0, sd =1, count =3)) %do% {
  max(i)
}
end1 <- Sys.time()
for_time <- end1 - start1
cat("Runtime using foreach:", for_time)

Runtime using foreach: 0.004794121

#RunTime parLapply
cl <- makeCluster(3)
registerDoParallel(cl)
start2 <- Sys.time()
results_seq <- foreach(i = irnorm(n= 5, mean =0, sd =1, count =3)) %dopar% {
  max(i)
}
end2 <- Sys.time()
par_time <- end2 - start2
cat("Runtime using parLapply:", par_time)

Runtime using parLapply: 0.04643011

#RunTime replicate
start3 <- Sys.time()
results_seq <- foreach(i = 1:3) %dopar% {
  replicate(3, rnorm(5))
  max(i)
}
end3 <- Sys.time()
rep_time <- end3 - start3
cat("Runtime using replicate:", rep_time)

Runtime using replicate: 0.01320696

cat("Runtime using foreach:", for_time)

Runtime using foreach: 0.004794121

cat("Runtime using parLapply:", par_time)

Runtime using parLapply: 0.04643011

cat("Runtime using replicate:", rep_time)

Runtime using replicate: 0.01320696
